code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of building‑related triples into a fluent, natural‑language
    sentence (or sentences) by grouping information per subject and using
    concise phrasing.
    """
    # Mapping for generic predicates
    verb_map = {
        "architecturalStyle": "has an architectural style of",
        "buildingStartDate": "started construction in",
        "completionDate": "was completed in",
        "floorArea": "covers a floor area of",
        "architect": "was designed by",
        "owner": "is owned by",
        "formerName": "was formerly known as",
        "buildingType": "is a",
        "developer": "was developed by",
        "tenant": "has a tenant",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "country": "is in",
        "currentTenants": "currently houses",
        "address": "has the address",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "region": "is in the region of",
        "state": "is in the state of",
        "website": "has website",
        "yearOfConstruction": "was constructed in",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "has NRHP reference number",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "was added to the NRHP on",
        "significantBuilding": "is known for the building",
        "governingBody": "is governed by",
        "leader": "is led by",
        "chancellor": "has chancellor",
        "governmentType": "has government type",
        "capital": "has capital",
        "language": "speaks",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title",
        "currency": "uses currency",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group",
        "origin": "originates from",
        "significantProject": "is known for the project",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in",
        "demonym": "has demonym",
        "keyPerson": "has key person",
        "architecture": "features architecture",
        "postalCode": "has postal code",
        "cost": "costs",
    }

    # Helper to clean object strings
    def clean_obj(obj: str) -> str:
        obj = obj.strip()
        if obj.startswith('"') and obj.endswith('"'):
            obj = obj[1:-1]
        # Remove parenthetical units, keep the numeric part
        if "(" in obj:
            obj = obj.split("(")[0].strip()
        return obj

    # Group clauses by subject
    subject_map: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    for triple in triples:
        subj = triple.subject
        pred = triple.predicate
        obj = clean_obj(triple.object)

        # Build predicate‑specific phrase
        if pred == "location":
            phrase = f"located in {obj}"
        elif pred == "buildingType":
            phrase = f"is a {obj}"
        elif pred == "height":
            phrase = f"rises to a height of {obj} metres"
        elif pred == "floorCount":
            phrase = f"has {obj} floors"
        elif pred == "numberOfRooms":
            phrase = f"has {obj} rooms"
        elif pred == "bedCount":
            phrase = f"has {obj} beds"
        elif pred == "country":
            phrase = f"is in {obj}"
        else:
            verb = verb_map.get(pred, "has")
            phrase = f"{verb} {obj}"

        subject_map.setdefault(subj, []).append(phrase)

    # Assemble sentences
    sentences = []
    for subj, parts in subject_map.items():
        if not parts:
            sentences.append(f"{subj}.")
            continue
        # Join parts with commas, add "and" before last part if more than one
        if len(parts) == 1:
            body = parts[0]
        else:
            body = ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + ", and " + parts[-1]
        sentence = f"{subj} {body}."
        # Capitalize first character
        sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]
        sentences.append(sentence)

    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
